orbyts_black_hole_shadows package¶

Submodules¶

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.orbits module¶

Generates photon orbits around a black hole.

class orbyts_black_hole_shadows.orbits.Orbit(b, D, metric, n_points=100000, verbose=False)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class to represent a photon orbit around a black hole.

b¶

Impact parameter of the photon.

Type:

float

D¶

Distance of the observer from the black hole.

Type:

float

metric¶

The metric describing the black hole spacetime

Type:

Metric

n_points¶

Number of points to generate in the orbit. The default is 100000.

Type:

int, optional

phi¶

Array of phi values along the orbit.

Type:

array

y¶

Array of r and dr/dphi values along the orbit.

Type:

array

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.orbits.generate_orbit(b, D, metric, n_points=100000, verbose=False)[source]¶

Generates a photon orbit around a black hole

Parameters:

  • b (float) – Impact parameter of the photon.

  • D (float) – Distance of the observer from the black hole.

  • metric (Metric) – The metric describing the black hole spacetime

  • n_points (int, optional) – Number of points to generate in the orbit. The default is 100000.

  • verbose (bool, optional) – Whether to print the fate (capture or escape) of each photon. The default is False.

Returns:

  • phi (array) – Array of phi values along the orbit.

  • y (array) – Array of r and dr/dphi values along the orbit.

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.plotting module¶

Functionality for plotting black hole shadows.

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.plotting.plot_orbits(orbits, xlim=(-5, 5), ylim=(-5, 5), add_box_size=False, box_size=0, imsave='', show=True)[source]¶

Plots the orbits of photons around a black hole.

Parameters:

  • orbits (list of Orbit objects) – List of Orbit objects to plot.

  • xlim (tuple, optional) – Limits for the x-axis. The default is (-5,5).

  • ylim (tuple, optional) – Limits for the y-axis. The default is (-5,5).

  • add_box_size (bool, optional) – Whether to add a box representing the box size used for the source. The default is False.

  • box_size (float, optional) – Size of the box to add if add_box_size is True. The default is 0

  • imsave (string, optional) – Path to save the plot to. The default is ‘’, not saving anything

  • show (bool, optional) – Whether to show the plot. The default is True

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.sources module¶

Contains classes representing sources and the main functionality for simulating black hole shadows.

class orbyts_black_hole_shadows.sources.Box(box_size, box_points, viewing_angles=(0, 0))[source]¶

Bases: object

Class to represent a box containing the black hole and the source.

box_size¶

The edge length of the box (in units of Rs).

Type:

float

box_points¶

The total number of gridpoints along each direction in the box.

Type:

int

viewing_angles¶

A tuple (theta,phi) the rotation of the source within the box relative to its definition.

Type:

tuple

cartesians¶

An array representing all the gridpoints in the box in Cartesian coordinates. e.g. cartesians[x,y,z] = -box_size / 2 + box_size/box_points * [x,y,z]

Type:

4D array

shericals¶

The gridpoints in spherical polar coordinates.

Type:

4D array

cylindricals¶

The grid points in cylindrical polar coordinates.

Type:

4D array

source¶

An array representing the gridpoints, with each entry being boolean value indicating whether that point is inside the source.

Type:

3D array

cartesians_source_frame¶

The gridpoints in the source frame (rotated by (theta, phi)) in Cartesian coordinates.

Type:

4D array

sphericals_source_frame¶

The gridpoints in the source frame (rotated by (theta, phi)) in spherical polar coordinates.

Type:

4D array

cylindricals_source_frame¶

The gridpoints in the source frame (rotated by (theta, phi)) in cylindrical polar coordinates.

Type:

4D array

orbits¶

A list of Orbit() objects representing the photon orbits calculated in the box.

Type:

list

b_values¶

The values of the impact parameter for which orbits have been calculated.

Type:

1D array

alpha_values¶

The values for alpha (the angle to the vertical on the screen).

Type:

1D array

pixels¶

Array of the pixel midpoints in (b, alpha) coordinates on the screen.

Type:

3D array

pixel_brightness¶

Array of shape pixels.shape[:2] storing the calculated brightness of each pixel.

Type:

2D array

add_source : Adds a source to the box.
clear_sources : Clears all sources from the box.
plot_source : Plots the source in Cartesian coordinates.
calculate_photon_rays : Calculates the photon rays through the box ending up on the screen.
plot_box_orbits : Plots the orbits of photons through the box ending up on the screen.
calculate_pixel_brightness : Calculates the brightness of each pixel on the screen based on the number of photons that hit the box and their impact parameters.
plot_image : Plots the image of the box on the screen based on the pixel brightness.
add_source(source)[source]¶

Adds a source to the box.

Parameters:

source (Source()) – A Source() object describing the source location

calculate_photon_rays(n_b, metric, D, b_max, n_points=100000, verbose=False)[source]¶

Calculates the photon rays through the box ending up on the screen.

Parameters:

  • n_b (int) – Number of impact parameters to consider for the rays.

  • metric (Metric()) – Metric() object to use for the calculations.

  • D (float) – Distance of the observer from the black hole (should be large enough to be in the asymptotically flat region).

  • b_max (float) – Maximum impact parameter to consider for the rays (should be a bit larger than the box size to capture all rays that could potentially hit the box).

  • n_points (int, optional) – Number of points to generate in the orbit. The default is 100000.

  • verbose (bool, optional) – Whether to print the fate (capture or escape) of each photon. The

  • False. (default is)

calculate_pixel_brightness(n_alpha)[source]¶

Calculates the brightness of each pixel on the screen based on the number of photons that hit the box and their impact parameters.

Parameters:

n_alpha (int) – Number of alpha values to consider. The number of pixels will be n_alpha x n_b.

clear_sources()[source]¶

Clears all sources from the box.

plot_box_orbits(imsave='', show=True)[source]¶

Plots the orbits of photons through the box ending up on the screen.

Parameters:

  • imsave (string, optional) – Path to save the plot to. The default is ‘’, not saving anything.

  • show (bool, optional) – Whether to show the plot. The default is True.

plot_image(n_plot_points=1000, smoothing=0, cmap='afmhot', kernel='linear', log=True)[source]¶

Plots the image of the box on the screen based on the pixel brightness.

Parameters:

  • n_plot_points (int, optional) – The amount of points to plot a brightness for along each direction on the screen. The default is 1000.

  • smoothing (float, optional) – The amount of smoothing to use for interpolating brightness values. The default is 0.

  • cmap (string, optional) – The colormap to use for the plot. The default is ‘afmhot’.

  • kernel (string, optional) – The kernel to use for the RBFInterpolator. The default is ‘linear’.

  • log (bool, optional) – Whether to take a log of the brightness values to ensure fainter features are clearly visible. The default is True.

plot_source(imsave='', show=True)[source]¶

Plots the source in Cartesian coordinates.

Parameters:

  • imsave (string, optional) – Path to save the plot to. The default is ‘’, not saving anything

  • show (bool, optional) – Whether to show the plot. The default is True

class orbyts_black_hole_shadows.sources.Source(condition, coordinates)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class to represent a source.

condition¶

A function taking a (3,) shaped array representing a point in space, returning a boolean depending on whether that point is in the source.

Type:

function

coordinates¶

The coordinates that condition is defined for. Choose from [“Cartesian”, “Spherical”, “Cylindrical”].

Type:

string

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils module¶

General Utilities for the black hole shadow code.

class orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.Metric(A, B, dAdr, dBdr)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class to represent a spherically symmetric, static metric.

A¶

The g_tt component of the metric, as a function of radius

Type:

function

B¶

1 / g_rr component of the metric, as a function of radius

Type:

function

dAdr¶

dA/dr

Type:

function

dBdr¶

dB/dr

Type:

function

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.cartesian_to_cylindrical(x)[source]¶

Converts Cartesian coordinates to cylindrical coordinates.

Parameters:

x (array-like) – Cartesian coordinates (x,y,z).

Returns:

Cylindrical coordinates (rho, phi, z).

Return type:

array

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.cartesian_to_spherical(x)[source]¶

Converts Cartesian coordinates to spherical coordinates.

Parameters:

x (array-like) – Cartesian coordinates (x,y,z).

Returns:

Spherical coordinates (r, theta, phi).

Return type:

array

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.cylindrical_to_cartesian(c)[source]¶

Converts cylindrical coordinates to Cartesian coordinates.

Parameters:

c (array-like) – Cylindrical coordinates (rho, phi, z).

Returns:

Cartesian coordinates (x,y,z).

Return type:

array

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.cylindrical_to_spherical(c)[source]¶

Converts cylindrical coordinates to spherical coordinates.

Parameters:

c (array-like) – Cylindrical coordinates (rho, phi, z).

Returns:

Spherical coordinates (r, theta, phi).

Return type:

array

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.spherical_to_cartesian(s)[source]¶

Converts spherical coordinates to Cartesian coordinates.

Parameters:

s (array-like) – Spherical coordinates (r, theta, phi).

Returns:

Cartesian coordinates (x,y,z).

Return type:

array

orbyts_black_hole_shadows.utils.spherical_to_cylindrical(s)[source]¶

Converts spherical coordinates to cylindrical coordinates.

Parameters:

s (array-like) – Spherical coordinates (r, theta, phi).

Returns:

Cylindrical coordinates (rho, phi, z).

Return type:

array
